mnemonics: NEW DELHI: National E-Waste Directive Enforces Landfill Handling Illegally
keyPoints: New Delhi is a major hub for informal e-waste recycling, posing significant health and environmental risks.
The city is central to India's policy development and challenges regarding e-waste management under the E-Waste (Management) Rules.
Key issues include the prevalence of child labor, toxic exposure from burning wires (for copper) and acid baths (for gold), and soil/water contamination.
commonMistakes: Assuming e-waste management is solely a government responsibility and not understanding the role of producers (EPR) and consumers.
Confusing informal recycling (prevalent in Delhi) with formal, regulated recycling channels.
Overlooking the specific health hazards (e.g., respiratory diseases, heavy metal poisoning) faced by informal sector workers in places like Seelampur.
